mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, consumer spending growth collapses and corporate earnings revisions turn negative, triggering immediate risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rising industrial production signals capacity utilization approaching limits, prompting inflation expectations and equity repricing that yield positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations when INDPRO rises during late-cycle regimes, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, mechanically supporting corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225.0, inflationary pressures force central banks to consider policy tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to aggregate output, signaling impending earnings downgrades that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy over baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, any month-over-month increase in industrial production confirms supply chain normalization and inventory restocking, triggering short-covering rallies that generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances during recovery regimes, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.
